key_components: Django DB setup fixture with session scope
Client fixtures (Django test client, DRF API client)
User fixtures (standard user, admin user, staff user)
Organization fixtures (basic org, org with owner)
Request context fixtures (API request factory, mock request)
patterns: UUID-based unique identifiers for test data
Factory pattern for flexible test data creation

key_components: Test markers (unit, integration, e2e, slow, api, model)
patterns: Comprehensive marker system for test categorization
Parallel test execution support via xdist

patterns_discovered.description: Well-organized fixture hierarchy covering all major app domains (users, orgs, menu, orders, subscriptions)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces test boilerplate and ensures consistent test data across test suite

patterns_discovered.description: make_user and make_organization fixtures enable custom test data creation while maintaining defaults
patterns_discovered.benefit: Tests can override defaults without reimplementing entire fixture creation logic

patterns_discovered.description: django_db_setup fixture with session scope initializes Plans and Roles once per test session
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures consistent reference data availability across all tests without per-test overhead

patterns_discovered.description: Three client fixtures: basic Client, APIClient, and authenticated APIClient
patterns_discovered.benefit: Supports testing different endpoint types (template views, REST API, authenticated flows)

patterns_discovered.description: pytest.ini defines comprehensive markers for unit, integration, e2e, and performance categorization
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables selective test execution (e.g., run only fast tests, only unit tests)

patterns_discovered.description: All test data uses UUID-based uniqueness for names, slugs, and emails
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents collisions when running tests in parallel or multiple times

gotchas_discovered.description: Fixtures like user, admin_user, staff_user have @pytest.mark.django_db decorator, but pytest-django documentation suggests using db parameter instead
gotchas_discovered.impact: May not be necessary; db parameter is the recommended approach
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Remove @pytest.mark.django_db decorators; db parameter is sufficient

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Nested fixture dependencies not fully validated
gotchas_discovered.description: Fixtures like staff_user depend on organization fixture, and organization_with_owner returns tuple instead of single value
gotchas_discovered.impact: Tests using organization_with_owner need to unpack tuple; inconsistent with other fixture return types
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document tuple unpacking requirement or consider returning named tuple/dataclass

gotchas_discovered.description: pytest.ini appears truncated in diff output (ends at '# Co' line 56)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Plugin configurations and additional settings may not have been fully captured
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Verify pytest.ini is complete; check file size and final configuration

gotchas_discovered.description: Model imports happen inside fixtures (within django_db_blocker) rather than at module level
gotchas_discovered.impact: Prevents circular import issues but makes fixture slower on first access
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document rationale; consider import at top if circular dependency resolved elsewhere

gotchas_discovered.description: Passwords like 'TestPassword123!' are hardcoded in fixtures
gotchas_discovered.impact: Not a security issue for test environment, but could leak if test data exposed
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider using Django's default_password or environment-based credentials

key_achievements: Created comprehensive conftest.py with 507 lines of production-ready fixtures
Configured pytest.ini with django-settings, markers, and plugin options
Established 20+ reusable fixtures covering users, organizations, menus, orders, and subscriptions
Implemented factory fixtures for flexible test data generation
Set up session-level database initialization for performance
Added support for parallel test execution via pytest-xdist
code_quality_indicators: Organized into sections with clear comments
Follows pytest best practices and conventions
Type hints present on fixture return types

recommendations.rationale: pytest-django best practice is to use db parameter for database access in fixtures

recommendations.recommendation: Add docstring note to organization_with_owner explaining tuple unpacking requirement
recommendations.rationale: Inconsistent return type (tuple vs single object) may confuse developers using this fixture

recommendations.recommendation: Verify pytest.ini is complete; file appears truncated in diff
recommendations.rationale: Important pytest configurations may be missing if file was cut off

recommendations.recommendation: Add fixture for authenticated user with organization membership for common auth flows
recommendations.rationale: Would reduce boilerplate in tests requiring authenticated org-scoped access

recommendations.recommendation: Consider caching Plan and Role lookups in django_db_setup to avoid repeated queries
recommendations.rationale: Multiple fixtures independently create/lookup same Plans; could be optimized

recommendations.recommendation: Extract hardcoded test credentials to environment variables or secure fixture configuration
recommendations.rationale: While not a production risk, follows security best practices

recommendations.recommendation: Add conftest.py fixtures index comment at top listing all available fixtures by category
recommendations.rationale: Large fixture file (507 lines) would benefit from quick reference guide
